title: SHINE Children and Youth Services - Learning and Social Support Programme
text:
SHINE Children and Youth Services - Learning and Social Support Programme - SupportGoWhere
SHINE Children and Youth Services - Learning and Social Support Programme
Share link
Provides literacy and language intervention to children aged between 7 and 12 years old (in mainstream schools) with learning difficulties.
SERVICE HIGHLIGHTS
Centre-based
Fees payable
Referral not required
SERVICE PROVIDERS
Find service providers near you
Collapse all sections
About this support

Provides literacy and language intervention to children aged 7 to 12 years (in mainstream schools) with learning difficulties.

Services include:
* Actualise Learning Potential and Promote Skills (ALPS) Language and literacy intervention programme - addresses the learning needs of children between 7 to 12 years old who typically lag 2 to 3 years behind their same-aged peers in language or literacy levels.
* Parenting sessions to equip and empower parents and caregivers to understand the needs of their children and better engage with them.
* Reading Odyssey - enhances the reading ability of children between 7 to 12 years old and to improve their motivation to read.

Who is this for?

Children aged 7 to 12 years (in mainstream schools) with learning difficulties.

What to expect?

The centre will conduct an intake assessment and consultation session with the child to better understand their needs and discuss follow up support and suitability for the programme.

How to apply?

You may contact the service provider directly for enquiries and to sign up for the programme.

Information last updated on 15 Sep 2025
